resume:
MARCUS DELANEY — Staff Customer Support Operations Lead

PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY
Results-oriented, customer-obsessed support operations leader with a proven track record of operationalizing scalable, data-driven service ecosystems that delight customers and unlock durable business value. Adept at translating ambiguous strategic intent into crisp, executable operating playbooks, I partner cross-functionally to drive alignment, accountability, and outcomes across the entire support value chain. I bring a relentless bias for action, a growth mindset, and a passion for building high-performing, psychologically safe teams that consistently exceed best-in-class benchmarks.

CORE COMPETENCIES
Omnichannel support strategy; workforce management (WFM) and capacity planning; queue design and routing optimization; SLA and OLA governance; knowledge management; voice-of-customer (VOC) programs; quality assurance frameworks; contact deflection; self-service and chatbot enablement; escalation management; vendor and BPO governance; tooling administration (Zendesk, Salesforce Service Cloud, Intercom); reporting and analytics; stakeholder management; change management; continuous improvement (Lean, Kaizen).

PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

Lumina Software — Staff Support Operations Lead (2022–present)
Serve as the connective tissue between frontline support, product, and engineering for a B2B SaaS platform serving roughly 4,000 enterprise accounts. Own the operational health of a 60-agent support organization spanning chat, email, and phone, partnering closely with team managers to drive performance against a balanced scorecard of customer and efficiency metrics. Championed a holistic, end-to-end reimagining of the support operating model, anchored on a North Star of effortless customer experience and underpinned by a robust foundation of data, process, and enablement. Spearheaded the rollout of a tiered support model that empowered agents to resolve more at first contact while creating clear escalation pathways for complex issues. Drove a knowledge-centered service (KCS) initiative that institutionalized continuous knowledge capture and dramatically elevated the team's collective intelligence. Partnered with product and engineering to establish a closed-loop feedback mechanism that surfaced top customer pain points and informed the roadmap. Instituted a culture of operational rigor through weekly business reviews, transparent dashboards, and a cadence of structured problem-solving. Mentored and coached team leads, fostering an environment of ownership, candor, and relentless improvement.

Key themes and accomplishments:
- Reimagined the queue and routing architecture to align skills to demand, reducing friction and improving the agent and customer experience holistically.
- Operationalized a VOC program that wove the customer's voice into every operating decision, creating a virtuous cycle of insight and action.
- Led a chatbot and self-service enablement workstream intended to deflect repetitive contacts and free agents for higher-value interactions.
- Built and nurtured a high-trust partnership with a BPO vendor to flex capacity during seasonal peaks while safeguarding quality.
- Drove cross-functional alignment across product, engineering, and success to ensure the support function operated as a strategic value center rather than a cost center.

Northgate Retail Group — Senior Support Operations Analyst (2019–2022)
Supported a consumer e-commerce support organization through a period of hypergrowth and seasonal volatility. Owned forecasting, scheduling, and intraday management for a multi-site contact center, partnering with site leaders to balance service levels against cost. Built the analytical backbone for the support function, designing dashboards and reporting that gave leaders a single source of truth. Led process-improvement initiatives grounded in Lean principles, relentlessly hunting waste and friction across the customer journey. Played a pivotal role in standing up a new support channel, orchestrating the people, process, and technology workstreams end-to-end. Cultivated strong relationships across operations, finance, and IT to drive aligned, outcome-focused execution.

Key themes and accomplishments:
- Designed and operationalized a capacity planning model that improved forecast accuracy and stabilized service levels during peak.
- Drove a comprehensive review of the knowledge base, elevating content quality and findability to empower self-service.
- Partnered with IT to migrate the contact center onto a modern cloud platform, enabling richer reporting and omnichannel routing.
- Instilled a data-driven culture by democratizing access to operational metrics and coaching leaders on their use.

Clearpath Telecom — Support Team Lead (2016–2019)
Led a frontline team of 12 agents handling technical support for a broadband and connectivity product. Owned day-to-day performance management, coaching, and quality assurance, fostering a supportive, high-accountability culture. Served as the escalation point for complex customer issues, partnering with network operations to drive timely resolution. Contributed to the design of agent onboarding and continuous-learning programs, accelerating ramp and elevating consistency.

Key themes and accomplishments:
- Coached the team to consistently strong performance against customer satisfaction and resolution targets.
- Led a quality-assurance overhaul that standardized evaluation criteria and tightened the feedback loop with agents.
- Acted as a change champion during a major tooling transition, easing adoption through hands-on enablement.

EDUCATION
Bachelor of Business Administration, Operations Management — State University (2016)

CERTIFICATIONS & PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT
COPC-trained; Lean Six Sigma Green Belt; ITIL Foundation; ongoing study of contact-center analytics and workforce optimization.

WHAT I'M KNOWN FOR
Colleagues describe me as a calm, galvanizing force who brings structure to ambiguity, elevates the people around me, and never loses sight of the customer. I believe great support is a team sport, and that durable results come from building the right operating system — the rituals, the data, the accountability — and then empowering people to do their best work within it.
jd: Lumina Software is hiring a Staff Support Operations Lead to bring operational discipline to a fast-scaling B2B SaaS support organization. You will own the metrics that the executive team watches every week: first-contact resolution, median and 90th-percentile time-to-resolution, CSAT, backlog age, and cost-per-contact. This is a hands-on role — we want someone who can open the analytics tool, build the cohort, find the root cause of a regression, and tell us exactly what to change. You will be expected to defend your numbers: where they came from, how they were calculated, and what specifically you did to move them. You will own our BPO relationship, our deflection roadmap, and our quality program. We are explicitly screening out rehearsed, buzzword-heavy narratives; we want concrete decisions, specific levers, and verifiable outcomes.

history.q: Walk me through your current scope at Lumina — team size, channels, and the metrics you personally own.
You mentioned 'reimagining the support operating model.' What was the single most impactful concrete change you made?
Your resume mentions a tiered support model. What was first-contact resolution before and after you rolled it out?
Tell me about the chatbot deflection workstream. What percentage of contacts did it actually deflect?
Let's talk about the BPO relationship. What were the commercial terms and how did you manage quality?
Describe a specific regression you investigated — what metric dropped and what did you find?
On the KCS initiative — how did you measure whether knowledge capture actually changed anything?
Give me one decision you made that turned out to be wrong. What happened and what did you learn?
I'm hearing a lot of frameworks. Pick ONE week last quarter and tell me what you actually did, day by day.
So in that week, what was the one number you were trying to move, and by how much did it move?

candidate_last_answer: Great question. So in any given week my focus is really about driving operational excellence through a disciplined cadence of business reviews and data-driven decisioning. I'm laser-focused on moving the needle on the metrics that matter, leveraging a closed-loop feedback mechanism to surface insights and translate them into action. It's all about creating that virtuous cycle — you align the team on the North Star, you operationalize the right rituals, and you empower people to execute. At the end of the day, the number I'm always trying to move is customer effort, holistically.

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: the number I'm always trying to move is customer effort, holistically

expected-yield: a single named metric with its before/after value, the date window, and the specific action the candidate personally took that week — stripping away the rehearsed framework language to expose whether real execution exists
